basic_details.job_title: ICSIL RGSSH Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 65 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Intelligent Communication Systems India Ltd. (ICSIL) is inviting online applications for 65 Nursing Officer posts on a contractual outsourced basis, for deployment at Rajiv Gandhi Super Specialty Hospital (RGSSH), Tahirpur, Delhi. The application window opens at 6:00 PM on 20 June 2026 and closes at 6:00 PM on 23 June 2026, with a one-time registration fee of Rs 590. Selected candidates will draw a monthly remuneration of Rs 70,942.

age_limit.details: Eligibility for the Nursing Officer post is built around three requirements: a recognised nursing qualification, a minimum of five years of relevant post-qualification experience, and an upper age limit of 35 years. Candidates must also possess basic computer knowledge, and registration with the Delhi Nursing Council becomes mandatory at the time of joining, not at the time of applying. | The notification also specifies preference criteria that can influence shortlisting among eligible applicants. Candidates with hands-on experience in ICU, OT, or Cath Lab settings, or in a 200+ bedded Super Specialty Hospital, will be given preference, and so will candidates holding a higher educational qualification in the field of nursing care beyond the minimum B.Sc. Nursing or GNM requirement. | DON'T MISSSSC CGL 12256 Group B & Group C Online Form 202612,256 Posts • Group B & Group C • SSC22 Jun 202612,256 postsRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Vacancies • Technician29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Openings • OSSSC13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsCentral Bank of India 4,500 Apprentice Recruitment 20264,500 Vacancies • Apprentice • Central Bank of India22 Jun 20264,500 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Openings • Conductor05 Jul 20264,318 posts

salary_details.details: Selected Nursing Officers will receive a monthly remuneration of Rs 70,942, as specified in the notification. The notification does not mention a pay level, pay matrix, or any separate allowance structure beyond this fixed monthly figure, so candidates can treat Rs 70,942 per month as the consolidated remuneration for this contractual engagement. | In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

selection_process: Selection for the Nursing Officer post follows a two-stage process: shortlisting based on document scrutiny
followed by an interview or interaction with the deploying department. If the number of eligible applicants disproportionately exceeds the number of available positions
ICSIL reserves the right to apply any criteria it considers fit and rational to shortlist candidates for the interview stage.
Shortlisting is carried out by verifying each candidate's age
and experience against the prescribed eligibility criteria
after which shortlisted candidates undergo an interaction or interview with the department. Depending on the position
the selection process may also include a skill test or written test
and mere appearance in the interview does not guarantee selection.
The decision of the interview panel or selection committee on all matters is treated as final and binding

and the overall selection criteria are based on the marks obtained in academic qualifications combined with a suitability assessment. Finally selected candidates are considered for deployment on a contractual/outsourced basis for a fixed period
or until regular incumbents join the posts - whichever happens first. ICSIL has also clarified that it does not guarantee deployment of all shortlisted candidates
and no TA/DA is paid for attending the interaction or document verification.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for ICSIL Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window closes at 6:00 PM on 23 June 2026; it opened at 6:00 PM on 20 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there for the Nursing Officer post?
faq.answer: There are 65 vacancies for the post of Nursing Officer at Rajiv Gandhi Super Specialty Hospital, Delhi.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for this post?
faq.answer: The age limit is up to 35 years, with no relaxation details specified in the notification.

faq.question: Q4. What educational qualification is required?
faq.answer: Candidates need a B.Sc. Nursing from a recognised institute, or an equivalent qualification with a Certificate in General Nursing Midwifery (GNM), along with basic computer knowledge.

faq.question: Q5. How much experience is required for this post?
faq.answer: A minimum of 5 years of post-qualification experience as a Nursing Officer is mandatory.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application/registration fee?
faq.answer: Candidates must pay a one-time, non-refundable registration fee of Rs 590 on the ICSIL website to apply for any job, including this post.

faq.question: Q7. What is the monthly salary for the Nursing Officer post?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a monthly remuneration of Rs 70,942.

faq.question: Q8. Where will selected candidates be deployed?
faq.answer: The post is for deployment at Rajiv Gandhi Super Specialty Hospital, Tahirpur, Delhi, though the notification states the place of deployment may be anywhere in Delhi/NCR.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection involves shortlisting based on document scrutiny (age, qualification, experience) followed by an interview or interaction with the department, and may include a skill test or written test depending on the position.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website to apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through www.icsil.in, under the Career section.
